compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (criterion G6 — approach soundness / fitness-for-purpose). Two grounded findings for author consideration; the chosen approach (a gate-specific doc that cross-references the canonical docs/workflow-engine.md rather than duplicating it) is otherwise sound and defensibly best — no superior structural alternative, so approach-selection PASSES.

FINDING 1 (fitness-for-purpose; terminology error that would be documented verbatim):
The plan calls the enable/disable mechanism a "kill-switch" in both the What ("the global enable/disable kill-switch") and the first AC ("config (incl. the disable kill-switch)"). The parent epic 5fd2 EXPLICITLY settled the opposite: SC#4 reads "'rollback' is simply turning the preference off — an ordinary config preference, NOT a deploy-time kill-switch, and no separate rollback mechanism is needed." Since this is a docs task whose stated Why is honest documentation for a broad client base, documenting an ordinary opt-in config preference AS a "kill-switch" would mislead clients into expecting a deploy-time/rollback safety mechanism the gate deliberately does not have (and the default is OFF, which the plan also omits). Coaching: align the doc to the epic's settled semantics — describe it as a config enable/disable PREFERENCE that DEFAULTS OFF, not a kill-switch; reconcile or drop the "kill-switch" wording.

FINDING 2 (fitness-for-purpose; the doc must convey the honest cert semantics the epic flags as a correctness concern):
The plan lists "fail-open behavior" and "certification semantics" as separate topics but does not require the doc to CONNECT them. The epic treats this linkage as a real, G6/E6-class correctness concern: the round-5 critical review's "hollow signal — fail-open + advisory + --force certifies skipped/ignored" finding maps to G6, and the resolution (Design decisions revisited #2) is that the cert means "process followed, no red flags WITH coverage recorded — NOT 'perfect'." For the doc to be fit for its honest-documentation purpose, the cert-semantics section must state the bounded claim explicitly (what the signature does and does NOT attest, given fail-open coverage gaps, advisory-by-default non-blocking, and --force override) — otherwise a client reading the CI-rigor-signal section could over-trust the signature. The plan's existing "honest end-to-end time-to-first-work" AC shows the right instinct; recommend an analogous explicit honesty requirement on the cert-semantics + fail-open linkage. (Note: criteria[]=G6; severity/priority deliberately omitted per pass-1 protocol.)

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review note (T8 — LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe, overlay):

T8 APPLIES (the plan documents an LLM/agent system: the three-pass gate, the Pass-4 coach with its move-registry enum and output schema), but I probed for PROVEN structural gaps and found the referenced vocabularies/protocols/fallbacks are all fully specified in the sources this docs ticket correctly cross-references — so most of the T8 checklist PASSES. One light instruction-locality coaching item below.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 criteria this chunk satisfies):
- No undefined-but-referenced enum: the MOVE REGISTRY value vocabulary the docs must cover is fully enumerated (moves 1-9,11,12 with id/triggers/template/source) in both child `75a9` and parent `5fd2` — the author is not being asked to document an enum whose values are undefined. The Pass-4 output schema `{move_id, subject, finding_refs[]}` is likewise fully defined.
- Fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step IS specified at the source: the SUBJECT VALIDATOR's REJECT path (emit NO coaching for that finding — best-effort drop, never a retry-into-prose loop) is explicit in `75a9`, so "how coaching is delivered" can be documented end-to-end including the failure case.
- Decision-rule co-location: the config surface the docs must cover (risk posture, chunking, budget caps, per-criterion blocking, the disable kill-switch) and the cert semantics ("process followed, no red flags + coverage", material-event binding) are each defined in `5fd2` (SC#4 = the disable preference, default OFF; Attestation section = cert semantics), co-located with the schema they govern.
- No counter/state-increment ambiguity introduced by this docs-only ticket; the finding-`id` minting owner (orchestrator `ca03`) and consumers (`75a9`/`db7b`) are unambiguously assigned at the source.
- Referenced agents/skills/enums exist and are fully specified (tool-verified against `75a9`, `5fd2`, `6f2d`).

FINDING (one, instruction-locality / pink-elephant-adjacent — coaching, not a blocker):
- [ ] The doc deliverable juxtaposes "How the gate works (THREE-PASS)" with a separate "Pass-4 affirmative COACH" deliverable without instructing the author to reconcile the apparent contradiction FOR THE READER — a reader hitting "three-pass" then "Pass-4" will ask "is the coach part of the model or not?" The decision rule that resolves it ("Pass 4 is AFTER the gate, ADVISORY, NOT in the decision path") lives in `5fd2`'s three-pass section but is not co-located in this ticket's framing, so the author could faithfully write a doc that leaves the four-vs-three tension unexplained.
  - criteria: [T8]
  - location: 6c5c "What" — "How the gate works (three-pass)" bullet vs the "Pass-4 affirmative COACH `75a9`" bullet
  - evidence: ticket "What" says document the gate as "(three-pass)" AND separately "The Pass-4 affirmative COACH `75a9`"; `5fd2` three-pass section resolves it ("Pass 4 — coach (advisory; AFTER the gate, NOT in the decision path) ... NEVER alters the Pass-3 decision"), but the docs ticket does not direct the author to surface that reconciliation.
  - suggested_fix: Add an AC (or What clause) requiring the docs to state explicitly that the gate's DECISION model is three-pass (evidence → binary-verify → deterministic gate) and that the Pass-4 coach runs AFTER the gate, is advisory-only, and never alters the Pass-3 block/advisory decision — so "three-pass + a Pass-4 coach" reads as intentional, not contradictory.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Report

**Verdict: FAIL**

Two acceptance criteria are not met: the workflow-engine usage doc cross-reference is absent (AC2), and the move registry content the ticket explicitly requires in the Pass-4 coach coverage is not documented (AC3).

---

### AC1 — Docs cover behavior, config (incl. the disable kill-switch), fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern
**MET.** All five topics are present in `docs/plan-review-gate.md`:
- Behavior (three-pass verdict model): lines 45–78 and the §"The verdict model — three passes + a coach" table.
- Config (enable/disable preference, defaults off): lines 145–166 — the table row states "Turning it off is the rollback — an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed" with code examples for both config file formats.
- Fail-open: lines 168–179 — abstain-on-error, DET-only degradation when LLM is unavailable, claim gate closes on signing-subsystem failure.
- Cert semantics: lines 123–143 — attestation means "process followed, no blocking red flags, with coverage recorded — *not* 'perfect'"; bounded claim explicitly stated.
- CI pattern: lines 193–198 — `rebar verify-signature`, certified-at-HEAD predicate, claimed-without-signature as the durable skip signal.

### AC2 — Cross-references the workflow-engine usage doc
**NOT MET.** The ticket requires cross-referencing the workflow-engine usage doc from epic 6f2d (WS-DOC), whose deliverable is `docs/workflow-engine.md`. That file does not exist anywhere in the repository (confirmed by listing `docs/` and searching `workflow-engine.md` across the repo). `docs/plan-review-gate.md` contains no mention of "6f2d", "WS-DOC", "workflow-engine", or any equivalent link. The existing implementation cross-references ([llm-framework.md](llm-framework.md), [reuse-surface.md](reuse-surface.md), [grounding.md](grounding.md), [event-schema.md](event-schema.md)) do not include the workflow-engine usage doc.

### AC3 — Docs cover the Pass-4 affirmative coach (the move registry + how coaching is delivered + advisory-only)
**NOT MET (move registry not documented).** `docs/plan-review-gate.md` line 77 describes Pass-4 at a high level: "maps each to a move from a locked registry; the coaching prose is rendered **deterministically** from the move's template (the LLM only picks the move + names a bounded noun-phrase subject — validated)." How coaching is delivered (deterministic rendering from template + subject) and that it is advisory (runs after the gate, never alters the Pass-3 decision) are covered. However, the ticket AC explicitly requires "the move registry" to be documented — the 11 named moves (1 spike/tracer-bullet · 2 prior-art/OSS · 3 pre-mortem · 4 riskiest-assumption-test · 5 weigh-alternatives · 6 specification-by-example · 7 thin-vertical-slice · 8 ADR/one-way-door · 9 plan-the-verification · 11 propagate-revisions-to-children · 12 generalize-the-finding, each with id/triggers/template/source) per ticket 75a9. The doc does not enumerate, name, or cross-reference the move registry entries. A search for "move_id", "move 1", "spike", "pre-mortem", "thin-vertical", and "advisory-only" in `docs/plan-review-gate.md` returns no matches.

### AC4 — States end-to-end time-to-first-work honestly (not just the 50ms claim check)
**MET.** `docs/plan-review-gate.md` lines 200–209 contain a dedicated "End-to-end time-to-first-work (honest)" section that explicitly distinguishes the fast local claim check (~50 ms, HMAC-only) from the out-of-band `review-plan` run (seconds to minutes depending on ticket size + tier) and the edit→re-review convergence loop (~2–3 rounds). No upfront wall-clock benchmark is claimed.

---

**Summary:** The core gate behavior, configuration, fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern, coaching delivery mechanism, advisory posture, and honest latency are all documented in `docs/plan-review-gate.md`. Two requirements are not met: (1) `docs/workflow-engine.md` does not yet exist and the gate doc contains no cross-reference to it or to 6f2d WS-DOC; (2) the move registry itself (the 11 named moves with id/triggers/template/source) is not documented — the doc references "a locked registry" but does not enumerate or cross-reference its contents.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion G6 (approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) [overlay]

VERDICT: one coaching finding (non-blocking) + affirmations.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this chunk satisfies):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: For a docs deliverable the load-bearing correctness risk is honesty/completeness/non-divergence. The plan's mechanism (author docs covering behavior / config / fail-open / cert semantics / CI pattern, cross-referencing 6f2d WS-DOC) is logic-complete, and AC#4 ("States end-to-end time-to-first-work honestly (not just the 50ms claim check)") explicitly forecloses the misleading-latency anti-pattern 5fd2's round-5 review flagged (presenting the ~50ms fast-claim number as user-facing time-to-first-work while the heavy review runs out-of-band, seconds-to-minutes). The canonical doc already realizes this honestly (docs/plan-review-gate.md §"End-to-end time-to-first-work (honest)", lines 231-240). Sound.
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: solves the named problem ("the gate serves a broad, diverse client base; it must be honestly documented") rather than a proxy — the ACs map to the topics requiring honest documentation, including the Pass-4 affirmative coach (move registry + how coaching is delivered + advisory-only), consistent with 75a9's advisory/single-turn/reference-by-id design (closed, signed).
- APPROACH SELECTION (alternatives generated, no negative priming): the only structurally-different choice for docs-only work is DUPLICATE-the-workflow-engine-content vs CROSS-REFERENCE it. The plan chose cross-reference ("the canonical workflow-engine intended-use doc lives in 6f2d"), the correct call — it avoids a divergence/duplication anti-pattern and respects 6f2d WS-DOC as the single source of truth. Defensible at-least-as-good on codebase-alignment, blast-radius, and maintainability; generated alternative discarded (not written into the plan).
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present: "the gate serves a broad, diverse client base; it must be honestly documented."

FINDING (G6 — fitness/mechanism for a docs artifact whose stated purpose is honest description):

- [ ] Reconcile the "kill-switch" terminology this doc plan promises against the SHIPPED disable mechanism before authoring, so the docs honestly describe behavior rather than misdescribe it. The What ("the global enable/disable kill-switch") and AC#1 ("config (incl. the disable kill-switch)") call the disable a "kill-switch," but the shipped mechanism is an ORDINARY per-project config PREFERENCE explicitly defined as NOT a kill-switch: `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` (default `false`), where "turning it off is the rollback — an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed" (src/rebar/config.py:259; docs/plan-review-gate.md:180; 5fd2 SC#4: "an ordinary config preference, not a deploy-time kill-switch"). Documenting a "global"/deploy-time-flavored "kill-switch" when the system ships a per-project preference is precisely the docs-vs-behavior divergence a docs deliverable must avoid (the gate's own round-5 self-review flagged this exact terminology drift: docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/final_gate_cycle3.json:312-326). Either (a) document the disable AS the `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` preference with its real default-off / per-project semantics and drop the "kill-switch" framing, or (b) if a genuinely separate global kill-switch is intended, name where it ships — as written the term has no referent in the implementation.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan §What ("the global enable/disable kill-switch") + §Acceptance Criteria line 1 ("config (incl. the disable kill-switch)"); src/rebar/config.py:259; docs/plan-review-gate.md:176-197; 5fd2 SC#4
  - evidence: plan text says "kill-switch"; shipped mechanism is `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` (default false), documented as "an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed" (config.py:259 comment + plan-review-gate.md:180); 5fd2 SC#4 explicitly "not a deploy-time kill-switch"; 5fd2's own gate run flagged the same docs/behavior terminology divergence (final_gate_cycle3.json:312-326).

ANTI-FP: documentation authoring is a well-understood activity with no genuine structurally-different "better approach" to coach beyond the duplicate-vs-cross-reference question the plan already handles correctly; G6's alternative-generation requirement is otherwise not-applicable here. The one finding above is a real fitness/honesty defect for an artifact whose stated job is honest description, not a manufactured design choice.

comments.body:
PASS-1 review (G6 — approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) [overlay]. One coaching finding (non-blocking) + affirmations.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria this chunk satisfies):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: for a docs deliverable the load-bearing correctness risk is honesty/completeness/non-divergence, and the plan's mechanism (document behavior/config/fail-open/cert-semantics/CI-pattern + the Pass-4 coach, cross-referencing 6f2d WS-DOC rather than duplicating it) is logic-complete. AC#4 ("States end-to-end time-to-first-work honestly — not just the 50ms claim check") explicitly forecloses the misleading-latency anti-pattern the epic's round-5 review flagged. No ordering/atomicity/concurrency concerns apply to a docs artifact.
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: solves the named problem ("the gate serves a broad, diverse client base; it must be honestly documented") rather than a proxy — the ACs map onto the topics requiring honest documentation, including the Pass-4 affirmative coach (move registry + delivery + advisory-only).
- APPROACH SELECTION (alternatives generated, no negative priming): the only structurally-different choice for docs-only work is DUPLICATE the workflow-engine content vs CROSS-REFERENCE it. The plan chose cross-reference ("the canonical workflow-engine intended-use doc lives in 6f2d"), which is the correct call — it avoids a divergence/duplication anti-pattern and keeps 6f2d WS-DOC the single source of truth. Defensibly at-least-as-good on codebase-alignment, blast-radius, and maintainability; generated alternative discarded (not written into the plan).
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present: the Why states why this fits — "The gate serves a broad, diverse client base; it must be honestly documented."

FINDING (G6 — fitness/mechanism for a docs artifact whose stated purpose is honest description):
- [ ] Reconcile the "kill-switch" terminology this plan promises to document against the SHIPPED disable mechanism before authoring, so the docs honestly DESCRIBE behavior rather than misdescribe it. The What ("the global enable/disable kill-switch") and AC#1 ("config (incl. the disable kill-switch)") call the disable a "kill-switch," but the shipped mechanism is an ORDINARY per-project config PREFERENCE explicitly defined as NOT a kill-switch: `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` (default `false`), whose source comment reads "turning it off is the rollback (an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed)" (src/rebar/config.py:259). The parent epic 5fd2 SC#4 settles the same point: "'rollback' is simply turning the preference off — an ordinary config preference, not a deploy-time kill-switch, and no separate rollback mechanism is needed." Documenting a "global"/deploy-time-flavored "kill-switch" when the system ships a per-project, default-OFF preference is exactly the docs-vs-behavior divergence a docs deliverable must avoid (5fd2's own round-5 self-review flagged this terminology drift). Either (a) document the disable AS the `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` preference with its real default-off / per-project semantics and drop the "kill-switch" framing, or (b) if a genuinely separate global kill-switch is intended, name where it ships — as written the term has no referent in the implementation.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: plan §What ("the global enable/disable kill-switch") + §Acceptance Criteria line 1 ("config (incl. the disable kill-switch)"); src/rebar/config.py:259; 5fd2 SC#4
  - evidence: plan text says "kill-switch"; shipped mechanism is `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` (default false), documented in source as "an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed" (config.py:253-259); 5fd2 SC#4 explicitly "not a deploy-time kill-switch."

ANTI-FP: documentation authoring is a well-understood activity with no genuine structurally-different "better approach" to coach beyond the duplicate-vs-cross-reference question the plan already handles correctly; G6's alternative-generation requirement is otherwise not-applicable here. The one finding above is a real fitness/honesty defect for an artifact whose stated job is honest description, not a manufactured design choice. (Severity/confidence/priority deliberately omitted per pass-1 protocol.)

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Docs: docs/plan-review-gate.md (two surfaces, three-pass+coach, Pass-3 math, advisory cap, proportionate scrutiny, attestation/freshness/invalidation, config, fail-open, sidecar, CI signal, honest time-to-first-work) + docs/reuse-surface.md + CLAUDE.md protocols. REMEDIATED: now ENUMERATES the full move registry (11 moves + templates + .rebar extension + C1 validator) and CROSS-REFERENCES the workflow-engine usage docs (workflow-authoring-v2/-editor; consolidated docs/workflow-engine.md is the pending 6f2d deliverable).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
## Completion Verification Report

**Verdict: PASS**

All four acceptance criteria are met by `docs/plan-review-gate.md`.

---

### AC1 — Docs cover behavior, config (incl. the disable kill-switch), fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern
**MET.**
- **Behavior (three-pass + coach):** Lines 49–81, the "verdict model — three passes + a coach" section with the full Pass table.
- **Config (enable/disable preference, defaults off):** Lines 176–197, table row for `verify.require_plan_review_for_claim` explicitly states "Default off," "Turning it off is the rollback — an ordinary preference, no kill-switch needed," with config examples for both `.rebar/config.conf` and `rebar.toml`.
- **Fail-open:** Lines 199–210, four distinct fail-open behaviors documented (abstain-on-error, DET-only LLM degradation, broken check abstains, claim gate fails closed on signing subsystem failure).
- **Cert semantics:** Lines 154–174, attestation section with explicit bounded claim: "process was followed, no blocking red flags, with coverage recorded — *not* 'perfect'"; freshness and invalidation rules documented.
- **CI pattern:** Lines 223–229, `rebar verify-signature`, certified-at-HEAD predicate, claimed-without-signature as the durable skip signal.

### AC2 — Cross-references the workflow-engine usage doc
**MET.** Lines 21–24: "The gate runs **on the workflow engine** — see the workflow-engine usage docs [workflow-authoring-v2.md](workflow-authoring-v2.md) + [workflow-editor.md](workflow-editor.md) (the consolidated `docs/workflow-engine.md` is the pending 6f2d WS-DOC deliverable; these are the current authoritative refs)." Both `docs/workflow-authoring-v2.md` and `docs/workflow-editor.md` exist in the repository. The 6f2d WS-DOC relationship is explicitly named.

### AC3 — Docs cover the Pass-4 affirmative coach (the move registry + how coaching is delivered + advisory-only)
**MET.**
- **Move registry:** Lines 105–130, full registry enumerated in a table: all 11 moves (ids 1–9, 11, 12), each with id, name, and the exact template string `(rendered with {subject})`. Project-extensibility (`.rebar/plan_review_moves.json`) and the C1 subject validator also documented.
- **How coaching is delivered:** Deterministic rendering from the move's locked template; the LLM only picks the move id and fills a bounded noun-phrase `{subject}`; `_validate_subject` rejects code/imperatives/overlong subjects.
- **Advisory-only:** Pass-4 is structurally post-gate (it acts over "surviving advisory findings" from Pass-3); Layer 2 is described as "advisory coaching review — never blocks by default"; the table heading is "three passes + a coach," establishing the coach as outside the decision path.

### AC4 — States end-to-end time-to-first-work honestly (not just the 50ms claim check)
**MET.** Lines 231–240, dedicated section "End-to-end time-to-first-work (honest)" explicitly separates the fast local claim check (~50 ms, HMAC only, no LLM) from the out-of-band `review-plan` run (seconds to minutes depending on ticket size and tier), and the edit→re-review convergence loop (~2–3 rounds). "No upfront wall-clock benchmark is claimed."

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The gate serves a broad, diverse client base; it must be honestly documented.

## What
- How the gate works (three-pass), the config surface (risk posture, chunking, budget caps, per-criterion
  blocking, the global enable/disable kill-switch), fail-open behavior, the certification semantics, and the
  CI-check pattern. Cross-reference the workflow-engine usage doc (6f2d WS-DOC).

- The Pass-4 affirmative COACH `75a9` (crusty-herb-stand): how coaching is delivered (the move registry, reference-by-id, advisory-only).
## Scope
- Docs only; the canonical workflow-engine intended-use doc lives in 6f2d.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Docs cover behavior, config (incl. the disable kill-switch), fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern.
- [ ] Cross-references the workflow-engine usage doc.
- [ ] Docs cover the Pass-4 affirmative coach (the move registry + how coaching is delivered + advisory-only).
- [ ] States end-to-end time-to-first-work honestly (not just the 50ms claim check).
compiled_state.title: Docs: gate behavior, config surface, fail-open, cert semantics, CI pattern
